source: CIS IBM CLOUD FOUNDATIONS BENCHMARK V1.1.0
title: Ensure IBM Cloudant is only accessible via HTTPS or TLS Connections

input_row.source: CIS IBM CLOUD FOUNDATIONS BENCHMARK V1.1.0
input_row.title: Ensure IBM Cloudant is only accessible via HTTPS or TLS Connections
input_row.description: IBM Cloudant instances can only be connected to over TLS or HTTPS connections. This behavior is by default and is non-configurable.
input_row.rationale: With IBM Cloudant, all access is over HTTPS by default an no user action is needed to ensure all data in transit is encrypted. Impact: With IBM Cloudant, all access is over HTTPS by default with no action required by the user. There is no impact as TLS and HTTPS connections are automatic and not user- configurable.
input_row.audit: Since the default and immutable value is that all access to Cloudant is over HTTPS, the only auditable procedure is to verify access to a Cloudant instance over HTTPS works while access over HTTP fails. 1. Log on to your IBM Cloud account 2. Go to the Menu icon > Resource List to access your list of account resources 3. Click the Cloudant service you are interested in to open the service dashboard. 4. Select Manage from the left navigation menu 5. Select the Overview tab. 6. In the Deployment details section, ensure the External Endpoint and External Endpoint (preferred) fields have prefixes of https:// 7. Ensure you can connect to the instance over https:// by clicking on Launch Dashboard or running a curl against the endpoint listed. 8. Ensure you cannot connect if you change the https:// to http:// in the URL and should result in a response of > port 80: Connection refused Examples using command line tool curl: With HTTPS: $ curl https://3dde8a0f-19d8-4698-a70e-e4757714a180-bluemix.cloudant.com {"couchdb":"Welcome","version":"2.1.1","vendor":{"name":"IBM Cloudant","version":"8158","variant":"paas"},"features":["geo","access- ready","iam","partitioned","pluggable-storage- engines","scheduler"],"features_flags":["partitioned"]} Without HTTPS: $ curl http://3dde8a0f-19d8-4698-a70e-e4757714a180-bluemix.cloudant.com curl: (7) Failed to connect to 3dde8a0f-19d8-4698-a70e-e4757714a180- bluemix.cloudant.com port 80: Connection refused
input_row.remediation: No remediation procedure. Impossible for end-user to impact this control in a negative way. Default Value: With IBM Cloudant, all access is over HTTPS by default with no action required by the user.
input_row.impact: With IBM Cloudant, all access is over HTTPS by default with no action required by the user. There is no impact as TLS and HTTPS connections are automatic and not user- configurable.

step1_initial_assessment:
**AUDIT APPROACH:** Manual

**APPROACH DECISION:**
The control for ensuring that IBM Cloudant is only accessible via HTTPS or TLS connections is described as default and immutable, meaning there is no configurable setting that users can alter. The provided audit procedure involves manual verification steps to check the URL endpoints for HTTPS prefixes and to test connectivity through these endpoints, ensuring that HTTP access is refused. Since this requires accessing the IBM Cloud console and manually testing connectivity via tools like curl, it is determined to be a manual process.

The instruction for checking these configurations explicitly mentions using a browser to navigate through the IBM Cloud console and manually verifying the endpoints, as well as using a testing tool (curl) to confirm connectivity behavior. These methods do not directly leverage any IBM Cloud API or CLI commands which could automate the process, thus making this control primarily reliant on human judgment and manual verification steps.

**MANUAL STEPS:**

1. Log in to your IBM Cloud account.
2. Navigate to the Menu icon > Resource List to access your list of account resources.
3. Click the Cloudant service you are interested in to open the service dashboard.
4. Select the "Manage" option from the left navigation menu.
5. Under the "Overview" tab, locate the "Deployment details" section.
6. Verify the External Endpoint and External Endpoint (preferred) fields start with "https://".
7. Use a web browser or a command-line tool like curl to connect to the Cloudant instance via the HTTPS endpoint. Ensure you receive a successful connection response, indicating secure access.
8. Test HTTP connectivity by replacing "https://" with "http://" in the URL and attempt the connection again. A connection refusal or error should confirm that HTTP access is not allowed.

These steps are essential to confirm that Cloudant instances are secured by allowing only HTTPS connections and rejecting any attempts over HTTP, aligning with the control requirements from the CIS IBM Cloud Foundations Benchmark.
